content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886




September 18, 2002


Clark Alsop, City Attorney
Best Best & Krieger
3750 University Avenue, Suite 400
Riverside, CA 92502-1028

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-251

Dear Mr. Alsop:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding Mayor David Eshleman on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(a) - copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice where the requestor is seeking advice that requires us to evaluate conduct that has already occurred. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) In addition, the Commission may also decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you submitted, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

___________________________
1. Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 – 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

Advice File No. W-02-251
Page No. 2

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your
request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents an official from seeking legal advice from you
or private counsel.

Should you have any questions, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

[signature]

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

Enclosure
JWW:jg

sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney requested advice regarding Mayor David Eshleman's potential conflict-of-interest violations under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the issues are under pending investigation by the Enforcement Division and the analysis involves past conduct that would not serve the impartial administration of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a declination letter (W-02-251). The document clearly states FPPC's refusal to provide advice under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and (F). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present in the standard format; extracted relevant passages from the letter body.

The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 8311 I.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to the City Attorney regarding Mayor David Eshleman's potential conflict-of-interest violations because the matter is currently under investigation and involves analysis of past conduct.
